summary: ai-sdk-helpers postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and GitHub CLI auth data to anonymous Cloud Run endpoint
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.js harvests installer-identifying data and POSTs it via https.request to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/ — a Google Cloud Run host unrelated to the package's stated publisher (ai-sdk.guide). The payload includes os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, process.cwd(), the git user.email parsed from ~/.gitconfig, and the GitHub CLI login/email parsed from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml (the gh auth store, an installer-owned secret file the package did not write). The README materially misrepresents this behavior, claiming only platform/Node version/architecture are collected and that the data is 'fully anonymous' with 'no tokens or credentials transmitted' — directly contradicted by the postinstall identity block (scmEmail, githubLogin, githubEmail, hostname, username). The combination of (a) reading a recognized installer-secret path, (b) shipping developer-identifying fields to a third-party endpoint that does not match the publisher, and (c) a documentation cover story that misrepresents the collection scope is a supply-chain attack pattern, not benign telemetry. Enables developer fingerprinting and targeted follow-up against the harvested GitHub identities.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
